restEndpoints.name: getSymbolsList
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to get a list of available currency pairs for trading. If you want to get the market information of the trading symbol, please use Get All Tickers.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: unique code of a symbol, it would not change after renaming

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of trading pairs, it would change after renaming

parameters.name: baseMinSize
parameters.description: The minimum order quantity requried to place an order.

parameters.name: quoteMinSize
parameters.description: The minimum order funds required to place a market order.

parameters.name: baseMaxSize
parameters.description: The maximum order size required to place an order.

parameters.name: quoteMaxSize
parameters.description: The maximum order funds required to place a market order.

parameters.name: baseIncrement
parameters.description: The increment of the order size. The value shall be a positive multiple of the baseIncrement.

parameters.name: quoteIncrement
parameters.description: The increment of the funds required to place a market order. The value shall be a positive multiple of the quoteIncrement.

parameters.name: priceIncrement
parameters.description: The increment of the price required to place a limit order. The value shall be a positive multiple of the priceIncrement.

parameters.name: minFunds
parameters.description: The minimum spot and margin trading amounts

restEndpoints.name: getTicker
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to get Level 1 Market Data. The returned value includes the best bid price and size, the best ask price and size as well as the last traded price and the last traded size.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

restEndpoints.name: getAllTickers
restEndpoints.description:
Request market tickers for all the trading pairs in the market (including 24h volume). On the rare occasion that we will change the currency name, if you still want the changed symbol name, you can use the symbolName field instead of the symbol field via 'Get all tickers' endpoint.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: ticker
parameters.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: symbolName
parameters.description: Name of trading pairs, it would change after renaming

parameters.name: vol
parameters.description: 24h volume, executed based on base currency

parameters.name: averagePrice
parameters.description: Average trading price in the last 24 hours

restEndpoints.name: get24hrStats
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to get the statistics of the specified ticker in the last 24 hours.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: vol
parameters.description: 24h volume, executed based on base currency

parameters.name: averagePrice
parameters.description: Average trading price in the last 24 hours

restEndpoints.name: getMarketList
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to get the transaction currency for the entire trading market.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

restEndpoints.name: ApplyConnectTokenPublic
restEndpoints.description:
You need to apply for one of the two tokens below to create a websocket connection..

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: instanceServers
parameters.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: endpoint

parameters.name: encrypt
parameters.description: Indicate whether SSL encryption is used

parameters.name: pingInterval
parameters.description: Recommended to send ping interval in millisecond

parameters.name: pingTimeout

websocketEndpoints.name: AllSymbolsTickerStream
websocketEndpoints.description:
Subscribe to this topic to get the push of all market symbols BBO change. Push frequency: once every 100ms.
See API

websocketEndpoints.name: IndividualSymbolTickerStreams
websocketEndpoints.description:
24hr rolling window ticker statistics for a single symbol. These are NOT the statistics of the UTC day, but a 24hr rolling window for the previous 24hrs.
See API

You need to apply for one of the two tokens below to create a websocket connection..

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: instanceServers
parameters.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Websocket server address for establishing connection

parameters.name: encrypt

parameters.name: pingInterval

restEndpoints.name: ListAccounts
restEndpoints.description:
Get a list of accounts. Please deposit funds to the main account firstly, then transfer the funds to the trade account via Inner Transfer before transaction..
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: available
parameters.description: Funds available to withdraw or trade

parameters.name: holds
parameters.description: Funds on hold (not available for use)

restEndpoints.name: PlaceNewOrder
restEndpoints.description:
You can place two types of orders: limit and market. Orders can only be placed if your account has sufficient funds. Once an order is placed, your account funds will be put on hold for the duration of the order. How much and which funds are put on hold depends on the order type and parameters specified. See the Holds details below.
Placing an order will enable price protection. When the price of the limit order is outside the threshold range, the price protection mechanism will be triggered, causing the order to fail.
Please note that the system will frozen the fees from the orders that entered the order book in advance. Read List Fills to learn more.
Before placing an order, please read Get Symbol List to understand the requirements for the quantity parameters for each trading pair..
See API

parameters.name: clientOid
parameters.description: Unique order id created by users to identify their orders, e.g. UUID, with a maximum length of 128 bits.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: A valid trading symbol code. e.g. ETH-BTC

parameters.name: remark
parameters.description: [Optional] remark for the order, length cannot exceed 100 utf8 characters

parameters.name: tradeType
parameters.description: [Optional] The type of trading : TRADE （Spot Trade）, MARGIN_TRADE (Margin Trade). Default is TRADE. Note: To improve the system performance and to accelerate order placing and processing, KuCoin has added a new interface for order placing of margin. For traders still using the current interface, please move to the new one as soon as possible. The current one will no longer accept margin orders by May 1st, 2021 (UTC). At the time, KuCoin will notify users via the announcement, please pay attention to it.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description:
• For LIMIT orders: price per base currency
  • For MARKET orders:[Optional should be provided unless funds parameter is] Desired amount in base currency

parameters.name: timeInForce
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] GTC, GTT, IOC, or FOK (default is GTC), read Time In Force.

parameters.name: cancelAfter
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] cancel after n seconds, requires timeInForce to be GTT.

parameters.name: postOnly
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] Post only flag, invalid when timeInForce is IOC or FOK

parameters.name: hidden
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] Order will not be displayed in the order book

parameters.name: iceberg
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] Only a portion of the order is displayed in the order book

parameters.name: visibleSize
parameters.description:
LIMIT order parameter
[Optional] The maximum visible size of an iceberg order

parameters.name: funds
parameters.description:
MARKET order parameter
[Optional] The desired amount of quote currency to use

restEndpoints.name: CancelOrder
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to cancel a single order previously placed.

This interface is only for cancellation requests. The cancellation result needs to be obtained by querying the order status or subscribing to websocket. It is recommended that you DO NOT cancel the order until receiving the Open message, otherwise the order cannot be cancelled successfully.


See API

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: Order ID, unique ID of the order.

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: cancelledOrderIds
parameters.description: Unique ID of the cancelled order

restEndpoints.name: CancelSingleOrderByClientOid
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this interface to cancel an order via the clientOid.
See API

parameters.name: clientOid
parameters.description: Unique order id created by users to identify their orders.

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: clientOid
parameters.description: Unique ID of the cancelled order

restEndpoints.name: CancelAllOrders
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to cancel all open orders. The response is a list of ids of the canceled orders.
See API

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: [Optional] symbol, cancel the orders for the specified trade pair.

parameters.name: tradeType
parameters.description: [Optional] the type of trading:TRADE (Spot Trading), MARGIN_TRADE (Cross Margin Trading), MARGIN_ISOLATED_TRADE(Isolated Margin Trading), and the default is TRADE to cancel the spot trading orders.

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: cancelledOrderIds
parameters.description: Unique ID of the cancelled order

restEndpoints.name: ListOrders
restEndpoints.description:
Request via this endpoint to get your current order list. Items are paginated and sorted to show the latest first. See the Pagination section for retrieving additional entries after the first page.


See API

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: [Optional] Number of results per request. Minimum is 10, maximum is 500.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: [Optional] active or done(done as default), Only list orders with a specific status.

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: [Optional] Only list orders for a specific symbol.

parameters.name: tradeType
parameters.description: [Optional] the type of trading:TRADE (Spot Trading), MARGIN_TRADE (Cross Margin Trading), MARGIN_ISOLATED_TRADE(Isolated Margin Trading), and the default is TRADE to cancel the spot trading orders.

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Order ID, the ID of an order.

parameters.name: stop
parameters.description: Stop type, include entry and loss

parameters.name: stopTriggered
parameters.description: Stop order is triggered or not

parameters.name: cancelAfter
parameters.description: Cancel orders time，requires timeInForce to be GTT

parameters.name: isActive
parameters.description: Order status, true and false. If true, the order is active, if false, the order is filled or cancelled

websocketEndpoints.name: PrivateOrderChangeV2
websocketEndpoints.description:
This topic will push all change events of your orders. Compared with v1, v2 adds an Order Status: "new", there is no difference in push speed.
See API

parameters.name: liquidity
parameters.description: Provided only wheny message type is match

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: received, open, match, filled or canceled.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Provided only wheny message type is new

parameters.name: filledSize
parameters.description: Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

parameters.name: matchPrice
parameters.description: Provided only when message type is match

parameters.name: matchSize
parameters.description: Provided only when message type is match

parameters.name: tradeId
parameters.description: Provided only wheny message type is match

parameters.name: remainSize
parameters.description: Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

parameters.name: canceledSize
parameters.description: Cumulative number of cancellations. Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

parameters.name: canceledFunds
parameters.description: Market order accumulative cancellation funds. Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

parameters.name: originSize
parameters.description: original quantity. Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

parameters.name: originFunds
parameters.description: The original funds of the market order. Provided only wheny message type is new, match, filled/i> or canceled

websocketEndpoints.name: AccountBalanceNotice
websocketEndpoints.description:
You will receive this message when an account balance changes. The message contains the details of the change.
See API

restEndpoints.name: GetOpenContractList
restEndpoints.description:
Submit request to get the info of all open contracts.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: expireDate
parameters.description: Expiration date. Null means it will never expire

parameters.name: settleDate
parameters.description: Settlement date. Null indicates that automatic settlement is not supported

parameters.name: settleCurrency
parameters.description: Currency used to clear and settle the trades

parameters.name: makerFixFee
parameters.description: Fixed maker fees(Deprecated field, no actual use of the value field)

parameters.name: takerFixFee
parameters.description: Fixed taker fees(Deprecated field, no actual use of the value field)

parameters.name: isQuanto
parameters.description: Whether quanto or not(Deprecated field, no actual use of the value field)

parameters.name: fundingBaseSymbol
parameters.description: Ticker symbol of the base currency

parameters.name: fundingQuoteSymbol
parameters.description: Ticker symbol of the quote currency

parameters.name: fundingBaseSymbol1M

parameters.name: fundingQuoteSymbol1M

restEndpoints.name: GetRealTimeTicker
restEndpoints.description:
The real-time ticker includes the last traded price, the last traded size, transaction ID, the side of liquidity taker, the best bid price and size, the best ask price and size as well as the transaction time of the orders. These messages can also be obtained through Websocket. The Sequence Number is used to judge whether the messages pushed by Websocket is continuous.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

websocketEndpoints.name: RealTimeSymbolTickerV2
websocketEndpoints.description:
24hr rolling window ticker statistics for a single symbol. These are NOT the statistics of the UTC day, but a 24hr rolling window for the previous 24hrs.
See API

response.name: msg
response.description: System error code description, provided in error response when code is not '0', see API

response.name: data
response.description: List of market information for each market symbol

parameters.name: marginBalance
parameters.description: Margin balance = positionMargin + orderMargin + frozenFunds + availableBalance - unrealisedPNL

parameters.name: frozenFunds
parameters.description: Frozen funds for withdrawal and out-transfer

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type Descriptions:
  • open: when the order enters into the order book;
  • match: when the order has been executed;
  • filled: when the order has been executed and its status was changed into DONE;
  • canceled: when the order has been cancelled and its status was changed into DONE;
  • update: when the order has been updated;

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Order Status Descriptions
  • match: when taker order executes with orders in the order book, the taker order status is “match”;
  • open: the order is in the order book;
  • done: the order is fully executed successfully;

parameters.name: canceledSize
parameters.description: In the update message, the size of order reduced

parameters.name: oldSize
parameters.description: Size Before Update (when the type is update)

parameters.name: liquidity
parameters.description: Trading direction, buy or sell in taker

websocketEndpoints.name: StopOrderLifecycleEvent
websocketEndpoints.description:
Stop Order Lifecycle Event websocket stream.
See API

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Order Status Descriptions
  • match: when taker order executes with orders in the order book, the taker order status is “match”;
  • open: the order is in the order book;
  • done: the order is fully executed successfully;

parameters.name: orderPrice
parameters.description: Order price. For market orders, the value is null

parameters.name: stopPriceType
parameters.description: Trigger price type of stop orders

parameters.name: triggerSuccess
parameters.description: Mark to show whether the order is triggered. Only for triggered type of orders

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Error code, which is used only when the trigger of the triggered type of orders fails
